MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SHANNON SEIBERT, Esq. of Seibert Bautista Montoya (Tel: 510-679-1981) for plaintiff;
Atty. MATTHEW K. YAN, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-3845) for defendant City and County of San Francisco and Neil Weingarten.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND NEIL WEINGARTEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

***** After the hearing, the Court signs an order denying defendants' motion for summary judgment and granting in part and denying in part defendants' motion for summary adjudication. (See signed order for complete ruling.) (302/RBU) *****

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And To Quash Second Notice Of Deposition Of Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Person Most Qualified
Off Calendar Sep-20-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Shannon Seibert, Esq; 510.679.1981; seibert@sbm.law, Joe Bautista, Esq, SEIBERT / BAUTISTA / MONTOYA for Plaintiff, Liza Williams
Matthew Yan, Esq; 415-554-3845; Deputy City Attorney; E-Mail: matthew.yan@sfcityatty.org; for
for Defendant, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

Plaintiff's counsel contests the Court's tentative ruling.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies in part and adopts in part the Court's tenative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, NEIL WEINGARTEN'S Motion For Protective Order is denied in part and granted in part. PMK topics are to be narrowed to any relationship between operations, staffing and responsibilities of each department to the termination or discipline of operators. Defendant's motion for a protective order with respect to the continued deposition of defendant Weingarten is granted to the extent of limiting further questioning to two additional hours. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Plaintiff's counsel is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

Matthew Yan, Esq. (415-554-3845; Matthew.Yan@sfcityatty.org) Deputy City Attorney for Defendants.

Counsel do not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and NEIL WEINGARTEN's Motion For Protective Order And To Quash Second Notice Of Deposition Of Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Person Most Qualified:

The parties agreed the categories of inquiry for the PMK notice at issue was overbroad and not sufficiently focused on the lawsuit factual issues. The Plaintiff requested the opportunity to file a sur-reply responsive to Defendant's reply and supporting declaration which request was granted. The sur-reply shall be served on or before the August 25, 2021. A further hearing is scheduled for August 27, 2021 commencing at 1:30 pm. The Court will send the Zoom invitation.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
